# Brotox Official
> The #1 resource for men's Botox. Education-first approach to male aesthetics with vetted provider matching across 38 US metros.

## About
Brotox Official is a men's aesthetics education and provider matching platform. We help men find vetted, board-certified Botox and aesthetic treatment providers near them. The service is free for consumers — we match men with providers based on location, treatment interest, and goals.

## Key Facts
- Founded: 2026
- Focus: Botox and aesthetic treatments specifically for men ("Brotox")
- Coverage: 38+ US metros, 600+ zip codes
- Providers: 65+ vetted, board-certified dermatologists and plastic surgeons
- Cost: Free for consumers
- Website: https://brotoxofficial.com
- Contact: contact@brotoxofficial.com
- Twitter/X: https://x.com/brotoxofficial

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is Brotox?
Brotox is the popular term for Botox treatments specifically for men. Men's facial anatomy requires different dosing and technique than women — typically 30-50% more units due to stronger facial muscles.

### How much does Botox cost for men?
Botox for men typically costs $300-$1,500 per session depending on the city, provider, and number of areas treated. Men generally need more units than women.

### Is Botox safe for men?
Botox has a 20+ year FDA-approved safety record with millions of treatments performed annually. Side effects are typically minor (temporary redness, slight bruising). Always see a board-certified provider.

### How long does Botox last for men?
Results typically last 3-4 months. Most men get 3-4 treatments per year. With regular use, some men find they can extend time between treatments.

### What areas do men get Botox?
The most common areas are forehead lines, frown lines (the "11s" between eyebrows), and crow's feet. Jawline Botox for jaw slimming and teeth grinding is also growing rapidly among men.

## Disclaimer
Brotox Official provides educational information and provider matching only. We do not perform medical procedures. All medical treatments should only be performed by licensed, board-certified medical professionals. Individual results may vary. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider before undergoing any aesthetic treatment.
